2603.18765.title: Implicit Grading Bias in Large Language Models: How Writing Style Affects Automated Assessment Across Math, Programming, and Essay Tasks
2603.18765.paper_summary: As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as automated graders in educational settings, concerns about fairness and bias in their evaluations have become critical. This study investigates whether LLMs exhibit implicit grading bias based on writing style when the underlying content correctness remains constant. We constructed a controlled dataset of 180 student responses across three subjects (Mathematics, Programming, and Essay/Writing), each with three surface-level perturbation types: grammar errors, informal language, and non-native phrasing. Two state-of-the-art open-source LLMs -- LLaMA 3.3 70B (Meta) and Qwen 2.5 72B (Alibaba) -- were prompted to grade responses on a 1-10 scale with explicit instructions to evaluate content correctness only and to disregard writing style. Our results reveal statistically significant grading bias in Essay/Writing tasks across both models and all perturbation types (p < 0.05), with effect sizes ranging from medium (Cohen's d = 0.64) to very large (d = 4.25). Informal language received the heaviest penalty, with LLaMA deducting an average of 1.90 points and Qwen deducting 1.20 points on a 10-point scale -- penalties comparable to the difference between a B+ and C+ letter grade. Non-native phrasing was penalized 1.35 and 0.90 points respectively. In sharp contrast, Mathematics and Programming tasks showed minimal bias, with most conditions failing to reach statistical significance. These findings demonstrate that LLM grading bias is subject-dependent, style-sensitive, and persists despite explicit counter-bias instructions in the grading prompt. We discuss implications for equitable deployment of LLM-based grading systems and recommend bias auditing protocols before institutional adoption.
2603.18765.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）在教育環境中越來越多地被用作自動評分工具，對它們評估中的公平性和偏見的擔憂變得至關重要。這項研究調查了在內容正確性保持不變的情況下，LLMs 是否會根據寫作風格表現出隱性評分偏見。我們構建了一個受控數據集，包含來自三個科目（數學、編程和論文/寫作）的180份學生回應，每個科目有三種類型的表面層擾動：語法錯誤、非正式語言和非母語表達。兩個最先進的開源LLM——LLaMA 3.3 70B（Meta）和Qwen 2.5 72B（Alibaba）——被要求在1-10的範圍內對回應進行評分，並明確指示僅評估內容正確性，忽略寫作風格。我們的結果顯示，在論文/寫作任務中，兩個模型和所有擾動類型均存在統計上顯著的評分偏見（p < 0.05），效應大小從中等（Cohen's d = 0.64）到非常大（d = 4.25）不等。非正式語言受到最嚴重的懲罰，LLaMA平均扣除1.90分，Qwen扣除1.20分，這在10分制中相當於B+和C+之間的差異。非母語表達分別被扣除1.35和0.90分。與此形成鮮明對比的是，數學和編程任務顯示出最小的偏見，大多數條件未能達到統計顯著性。這些發現表明，LLM評分偏見依賴於科目、對風格敏感，並且即使在評分提示中有明確的反偏見指示時仍然存在。我們討論了基於LLM的評分系統公平部署的影響，並建議在機構採用之前進行偏見審計協議。
2603.18765.author: Rudra Jadhav et.al.
2603.18765.authors: Rudra Jadhav, Janhavi Danve, Sonalika Shaw
